# audiobookshelf — GitOps manifests

Generated by [gitops-scaffold](https://github.com/06ninjatronics/gitops-scaffold)
from `tests/fixtures/compose/audiobookshelf-compose.yml`.

**Confidence: 65%** — review every item under "Needs
review" below before applying these manifests to a cluster.

> This project generates maintainable GitOps scaffolding, not automatically
> production-ready Kubernetes deployments. Every manifest here is meant to
> be readable, idiomatic YAML you'd be comfortable maintaining by hand —
> review it, don't just apply it blind.

## Namespace

`apps`

## Services

### audiobookshelf

- Image: `ghcr.io/advplyr/audiobookshelf:v2.35.1`
- Ports: 80/TCP (published on host port 13378 in Compose)
- Environment variables:
  - `TZ` = `America/New_York`
- Volumes:
  - `/audiobooks` → PVC `audiobookshelf-audiobooks` (1Gi)
  - `/podcasts` → PVC `audiobookshelf-podcasts` (1Gi)
  - `/config` → PVC `audiobookshelf-config` (1Gi)
  - `/metadata` → PVC `audiobookshelf-metadata` (1Gi)
- Port-forward:

  ```sh
  kubectl port-forward -n apps deploy/audiobookshelf 13378:80
  ```

## Validation

```sh
gitops-scaffold validate .
```

If `kubectl` is installed, `gitops-scaffold validate . --kubectl` additionally
runs `kubectl kustomize .` to confirm this directory builds cleanly.

## Skipped

- Nothing was skipped.

## Limitations

- Compose's `restart:` policy has no Deployment equivalent — Deployments
  always maintain their desired replica count regardless of it.
- Healthcheck timing fields approximate Compose's semantics but aren't
  identical (see `docs/generation.md`).
- PVC storage sizes and the exact UID/GID a container runs as are never
  guessed — see the review items above.
- `build:`-only services (no `image:`) aren't supported; that service's
  manifests are skipped entirely.

## Next steps

- [ ] Populate the real Secret(s) named above — affected Deployments will
      not become Ready until they exist.
- [ ] Review every `REVIEW REQUIRED` marker in the generated YAML.
- [ ] Add an Ingress if this needs to be reachable from outside the cluster
      (`gitops-scaffold generate ... --ingress-host ... --ingress-class ...
      --tls-secret ... --cluster-issuer ...`) and confirm TLS/DNS.
- [ ] Confirm each container's runtime user is non-root where possible.
- [ ] Test that persistent data actually survives a pod restart before
      relying on any generated PVC in production.
